title: Defining a Class
ques: Create a class named **`Car`** with public members `brand` (string) and `year` (int). Create an object of `Car` and assign values.
explanation: Classes are user-defined data types acting as blueprints for objects. `public:` makes members accessible from outside the class.

title: Class Methods
ques: Add a method (member function) named **`honk`** to the `Car` class that prints "Beep beep!" when called.
explanation: Methods are functions defined inside a class. They define the behavior of the objects.

title: Constructors
ques: What is a **constructor**? Add a constructor to the `Car` class to initialize `brand` and `year` when an object is created.
explanation: A constructor is a special method with the same name as the class. It is automatically called when an object is instantiated.

title: Access Specifiers
ques: Explain the difference between **`public`** and **`private`** access specifiers. Try to access a private member from `main` to see the restriction.
explanation: `public` members are accessible from anywhere. `private` members (the default access) are only accessible from within the class itself.

title: Encapsulation (Getters/Setters)
ques: Use **Encapsulation** to protect `salary` (private). Provide public methods `setSalary` and `getSalary` to access it.
explanation: Encapsulation bundles data (variables) and methods. Using getters and setters allows validation and control over data access.

title: Destructors
ques: Define a **destructor** for a class `Test` that prints "Object destroyed" when the object goes out of scope.
explanation: A destructor has the class name preceded by a tilde `~`. It takes no arguments and returns nothing. It cleans up resources.

title: Defining Methods Outside Class
ques: Define a method `display()` **outside** the class definition using the scope resolution operator `::`.
explanation: To define a method outside the class, use `ReturnType ClassName::MethodName() { ... }`.

title: Static Members
ques: Create a class `Counter` with a **static** integer `count` that tracks the number of objects created. Increment it in the constructor.
explanation: Static members belong to the class, not specific objects. There is only one copy of `count` shared by all `Counter` objects.

title: This Pointer
ques: Explain the **`this`** pointer. Use it in a method to differentiate between a member variable `x` and a parameter `x`.
explanation: `this` is a pointer to the current object instance. It is used to access members when parameter names shadow member names.

title: Copy Constructor
ques: Create a **copy constructor** for a class `Point` copying `x` and `y` coordinates. Demonstrate copying an object.
explanation: A copy constructor initializes a new object as a copy of an existing object. Signature: `ClassName(const ClassName &obj)`.
